mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate reduces discount rates and supports equity valuation multiples, so declining FEDFUNDS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating sharply.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative or below unconditional returns after FEDFUNDS declines, or if the sign reverses in a rolling 2-year window and persists for at least 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals weakening household income and slower future sales, so UNRATE increases above trend predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. This effect should appear when payroll growth is also slowing.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the hit rate does not exceed the unconditional baseline by at least 10 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in industrial production and retail sales indicates broad real demand acceleration, which should lift earnings expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal requires both series to rise together.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-positive state does not outperform the single-series states, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not higher when both INDPRO and RSAFS are rising versus when only one is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production signals weakening output and margin pressure, so falling INDPRO predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is most reliable when employment is not improving.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if below-trend INDPRO episodes do not show negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal remains positive for two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above trend indicate stronger construction activity, wealth effects, and domestic demand, which should support equity returns over the next month. The signal is stronger when the labor market is stable.
failure_condition: Reject if housing-start surges are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the average return after the trigger is not above zero.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation rises while the policy rate stays near zero, real rates fall and liquidity conditions ease, which should support S&P 500 returns over the following week. The mechanism fails if inflation acceleration is matched by a tightening response.
failure_condition: Reject if the triggered state does not produce positive 5-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if returns turn negative after the inflation-rate spread condition appears.

mechanism_narrative: A rising payrolls trend reflects stronger labor income and consumption capacity, so PAYEMS growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect is expected to weaken if unemployment is simultaneously rising.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average after consecutive payroll gains, or if the signal performs no better than random in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in retail sales and housing starts captures broad nominal demand strength across consumer spending and interest-sensitive sectors, which should lift equities over the next month. The signal breaks when one series strengthens but the other contracts.
failure_condition: Reject if positive joint-momentum episodes do not outperform single-variable episodes, or if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher when RSAFS and HOUST both rise versus when either one falls.
